functional_requirements.name: Full Page HTML Extraction
functional_requirements.description: Capture complete rendered page HTML with all DOM elements, attributes, and structure in raw, prettified, and minified formats
acceptance_criteria: Capture returns identical HTML regardless of timing (idempotent)
All DOM elements including Shadow DOM included in output
Inline scripts and styles preserved verbatim with proper escaping
HTML structure is valid and re-parseable
Handles edge cases: frames, iframes, web components, custom elements, portals
Provides three format options in single response
Response includes metrics: element count, total size, unique tag types
Supports selective capture: full page or specific subtree by selector
Includes comments and doctype declarations

functional_requirements.name: DOM Structure Snapshot
functional_requirements.description: Create structured snapshot of DOM tree with metadata about hierarchy and element properties including computed styles and visibility state
acceptance_criteria: DOM snapshot includes parent-child relationships for all elements
Computed styles captured (not just inline styles)
Element visibility state recorded (display, visibility, opacity, z-index)
Data-* attributes and custom element properties preserved
Form field metadata: type, name, value, required, validation rules
Detects and marks hidden elements
Returns hierarchical JSON with XPath for element location
Includes text node content with whitespace preservation option
Captures bounding box and scroll position for each element
Identifies dynamic content markers (data-reactid, ng-*, v-*, etc.)

functional_requirements.name: Complete JavaScript Code Extraction
functional_requirements.description: Extract all JavaScript code executing in the page context including inline scripts, external files, and dynamically loaded modules
acceptance_criteria: Extracts inline script tag content with original formatting
Captures external script URLs with integrity hash (SRI) when present
Identifies dynamically injected scripts (added via DOM manipulation)
Records script execution order and dependencies
Includes script tags with attributes (async, defer, module, nomodule, type)
Detects bundled code (webpack, rollup, parcel) markers
Returns source maps if available
Identifies minified vs. source code
Captures variable scope information when available
Records error callbacks and event listeners

functional_requirements.name: JavaScript Execution Context
functional_requirements.description: Snapshot JavaScript execution context at capture time including window state, functions, global variables, and loaded libraries
acceptance_criteria: Captures window object enumerable properties
Records defined function names and signatures
Identifies loaded libraries (jQuery, React, Angular, etc.)
Captures console output history (logs, errors, warnings)
Records event listeners attached to elements
Includes timers (setTimeout, setInterval) state
Captures localStorage and sessionStorage contents
Records IndexedDB database information
Handles circular references and non-serializable objects
Provides safe serialization for function contents

functional_requirements.name: Complete CSS Extraction
functional_requirements.description: Extract all stylesheets including inline blocks, external files, @imports, and computed styles applied to elements
acceptance_criteria: Extracts inline style tag content with original formatting
Captures external stylesheet URLs with integrity hashes
Detects @import directives and resolves imported stylesheets
Identifies CSS preprocessor files (SASS, LESS, PostCSS)
Records stylesheet load order
Includes media query conditions and specificity
Captures CSS variables (custom properties) and values
Records font imports (@font-face)
Identifies CSS-in-JS frameworks (styled-components, emotion, etc.)
Returns computed styles for each element

functional_requirements.name: Network Request/Response Capture
functional_requirements.description: Capture HTTP/HTTPS requests and responses with headers, bodies, timing, and status codes in HAR format
acceptance_criteria: Captures all HTTP/HTTPS requests and responses
Records request headers, method, URL, query parameters
Records response headers, status code, reason phrase
Includes request and response bodies (with size limits)
Records timing: DNS lookup, connect, SSL/TLS, wait, download
Captures query strings and POST form data
Records redirect chains
Identifies blocked requests (CORS, CSP, etc.)
Includes TLS certificate information
Records content-type and encoding
Supports filtering by resource type, domain, or pattern
Exports to HAR format compatible with tools
Records WebSocket connections and messages
Handles binary data appropriately

functional_requirements.name: TLS Certificate Extraction
functional_requirements.description: Extract TLS/SSL certificate information from secure connections including chain, subject, issuer, validity, and cipher suite
acceptance_criteria: Extracts server certificate (leaf certificate)
Captures full certificate chain to root CA
Records subject distinguished name (CN, O, C, etc.)
Records issuer distinguished name
Includes validity period (notBefore, notAfter)
Captures public key and key size
Records certificate serial number
Includes signature algorithm and hash
Records certificate extensions (SAN, EKU, etc.)
Captures cipher suite information
Records protocol version (TLS 1.2, 1.3, etc.)
Identifies self-signed certificates
Checks certificate validity and expiration
Records OCSP stapling status

functional_requirements.name: Multi-Format Export
functional_requirements.description: Export captured forensic data in JSON, CSV, XML, and HTML formats with hierarchical directory structure and integrity hashes
acceptance_criteria: Supports JSON format with complete data structure
Supports CSV format with flattened records
Supports XML format with schema validation
Supports HTML format with interactive viewing
Creates organized directory structure
Includes metadata files (timestamps, source URLs, capture method)
Generates integrity hashes (SHA-256) for all files
Creates manifest file with file inventory
Supports compression (ZIP, TAR.GZ)
Preserves data relationships across formats
Handles large files with streaming
Includes README with format documentation

functional_requirements.name: Batch Operations
functional_requirements.description: Perform forensic capture operations across multiple pages with parallel/sequential execution and progress tracking
acceptance_criteria: Accepts array of URLs for batch processing
Supports parallel execution (configurable concurrency)
Provides sequential execution mode
Records success/failure for each URL
Includes partial failure handling (continue on error)
Provides progress callbacks
Returns aggregated results
Supports output organization by URL
Allows capture type filtering per URL
Records execution timing per URL
Handles timeout and retry logic
Provides cancellation capability

functional_requirements.name: Encrypted Export
functional_requirements.description: Support encrypted export of sensitive forensic data using AES-256 encryption with password or key-based options
acceptance_criteria: Uses AES-256-GCM encryption
Supports password-based key derivation (PBKDF2)
Supports direct key provision
Includes authentication tag (GCM mode)
Records encryption metadata (algorithm, salt, IV)
Supports compression before encryption
Provides secure key management
Includes decryption instructions
Validates integrity after decryption
Supports selective field encryption

details: HTML capture completes in less than 2 seconds per page
DOM snapshot completes in less than 1.5 seconds
JavaScript extraction completes in less than 1 second
CSS extraction completes in less than 1 second
Network capture (HAR) completes in less than 2 seconds
Batch operation processes 10 URLs in less than 45 seconds (parallel)
No memory leaks during batch operations
Memory usage remains under 500MB for 1000-element pages
JSON export completes in less than 3 seconds for full page capture
CSV export completes in less than 2 seconds
Compression (ZIP) completes in less than 5 seconds
Encryption (AES-256) completes in less than 4 seconds
Large file handling (more than 100MB) uses streaming
100% of DOM elements captured without loss
HTML is character-for-character accurate
No content alteration or modification
All inline resources preserved
Whitespace and formatting preserved when requested
SHA-256 hash verification of all captured content
Chain of custody documentation without gaps
Timestamp accuracy within 100 milliseconds
All data verifiable against independent sources
Optional encryption for exported data (AES-256-GCM)
Secure handling of sensitive data (passwords redacted)
SSL/TLS for network capture
Audit logging of all export operations
Access control to forensic data
Cryptographic hashing of all evidence
Tamper detection capability
Chain of custody validation
Timestamp verification
Works with all 164 existing WebSocket commands
Compatible with existing evasion features
Compatible with proxy/Tor integration
Compatible with session management
Compatible with all 7 fingerprint profiles
HAR format compatible with HAR viewer tools
JSON schema-compliant output
CSV follows RFC 4180 standard
HTML renders in all modern browsers
ZIP archives compatible with standard tools
Python client library with intuitive interface
JavaScript/Node.js client library
Comprehensive examples for common tasks
Clear error messages with recovery suggestions
Documentation for all commands and parameters
Complete API reference with examples
5-minute quick start guide
Integration guide for common platforms
Troubleshooting guide
Best practices document

commands.description: Capture HTTP/HTTPS traffic in HAR format

commands.name: batch_capture_forensic
commands.description: Capture forensic data from multiple URLs
